method: substring match of content_words against search_raw titles/snippets/URLs; overlap>=2 forces HIT

anchor escapement.evidence: IEEE has 'anchor clock escapement' not exact substring 'anchor escapement'

interpretation: Prior art substantially overlaps; the escapement-as-bidirectional-feedback-regulator framing is explicitly described on Wikipedia. Novelty claim weakened: the analogy maps to a well-known horological concept, though the LLM-runtime application remains unaddressed by arxiv results returned.
skeptical_note: Q1 arxiv results show LLM scheduling literature (throttLL'eM, DTMC scheduling, Agent.xpu) but none use escapement/bidirectional-oscillator framing. The novelty gap is the LLM-application mapping, not the horology side.

disagreements_with_07.note: 07 uses agent judgment for same-problem-space; I keep this miss because zero substring overlap and the mechanism (predictive GPU freq throttle) is one-way, not bidirectional escapement.
DTMC scheduling is discrete-event but not bidirectional feedback-oscillator; substring overlap=0.
'Inference-time feedback' is not the same as bidirectional regulator oscillator; substring overlap=0.
07 counts 'anchor escapement' as substring in 'anchor clock escapement' — strictly false (word 'clock' intervenes). 07's forced-hit rule application is loose here.
